item.title: Kenya Inflation Hits Two-Year High as Iran War Shakes Up Economy
item.description: Kenya’s annual inflation climbed to its highest level in two years and is expected to accelerate further as fuel supply disruptions linked to the war in Iran continue to push up prices, according to its Treasury Secretary.

item.title: Junior Bankers Sick of Grunt Work Build $2 Billion AI Tool to Do the Job
item.description: Rogo, started by young bankers around a kitchen table in 2021, just won a multibillion-dollar valuation

item.title: EU Relaxes State-Aid Shackles to Offset Iran Economic Pressures
item.description: The European Union temporarily relaxed its state-aid regime to give nations more scope to subsidize firms hit hardest by higher energy and raw material prices amid the conflict in Iran.

item.title: US Has More Natural Gas Than It Can Use as War Chokes Global Supply
item.description: Soaring gas production is poised to bolster US manufacturing, creating an economic tailwind

item.title: Armani Posts 2025 Profit Rise as Higher-End Lines Lift Results
item.description: Armani Group managed to raise profit in 2025 despite a decline in sales, as full-price and high-end lines bolstered results at the Italian fashion house.

item.title: Ardian Helps Canada’s Largest Pensions Exit Buyout Funds
item.description: Ardian is increasingly a buyer of private equity assets from Canadian pension plans, as institutional investors turn to the secondary market to free up cash following a prolonged period of low deal activity.

item.title: Most Traders Lose on Polymarket and Winners Look Like Bots
item.description: Most profits made by a tiny number of accounts

item.title: Singapore Is Pulling In Dubai Gold as Middle East War Drags On
item.description: Imports of gold into Singapore from Dubai hit a record last month, thanks to wealthy investors seeking alternative hubs against the backdrop of an eight-week conflict in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: UBS Sees Recovery Signs in US Wealth Business as Inflows Return
item.description: UBS Group AG’s wealth-management business in the Americas showed tentative signs of revival after a period of client fund outflows and departures by key staff.

item.title: UBS Says Wealthy Clients Lost Some Interest in Private Credit
item.description: UBS Group AG Chief Financial Officer Todd Tuckner said that recent developments have cooled excitement among the lender’s rich clients about private credit, adding to the challenges facing the asset class.

item.title: PetroChina Reports Record Quarterly Profit on Higher Oil Prices
item.description: PetroChina Co. posted its best ever quarterly profit as China’s largest oil and gas producer benefited from rising energy prices due to the war in the Middle East.

item.title: AI Risks Will Widen Gap Between Chips, Software: Markets Pulse
item.description: A broad advance in technology stocks this earnings season will mask a growing divergence between software and hardware names, the latest Markets Pulse survey shows.

item.title: Kenya Economy Grows at Slowest Pace Since 2020 as Drought Bites
item.description: Kenya’s economy expanded at its slowest pace in five years in 2025, as drought weighed on the East African nation’s key agricultural sector.

item.title: Aging Oil Tanker Suggests Iran May Be Bringing Older Ships Back
item.description: An aging Iranian supertanker has appeared at Kharg Island after years off the radar, a sign that Tehran could be using retired ships to keep loading oil as its storage space runs out.

item.title: 80 Seconds of Big Tech Earnings Will Decide Stock Market’s Fate
item.description: Investors looking for clues on which direction the stock market is headed in the coming weeks will get a rapid-fire reading as soon as trading ends on Wednesday.

item.title: Adani’s Giant Copper Plant Hit By Technical Woes in First Year
item.description: Billionaire Gautam Adani’s $1.2 billion copper plant has hit a string of technical setbacks since it was commissioned ten months ago, raising concerns about the future of an operation vital to adding supply outside China.

item.title: Brazil Central Bank Set for Meek Rate Cut as Inflation Limits Options
item.description: Brazil’s central bank will likely deliver a timid interest rate cut for the second straight meeting as an energy price shock and fresh fiscal stimulus limit a window of opportunity to unwind tight monetary policy.

item.title: China’s Mega Banks Ramp Up Profit Growth in First Quarter
item.description: China’s largest state-owned lenders reported an up-tick in earnings growth in the first quarter, signaling a potential turn for an industry long defined by a protracted squeeze on profitability.

item.title: Nasdaq-Listed MakeMyTrip Said to Hire Banks for India Offering
item.description: MakeMyTrip Ltd., an online travel platform listed on the Nasdaq, is considering a listing in Mumbai, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Global Shocks Seen Spurring African Pivot to Domestic Capital
item.description: Rising geopolitical uncertainty is driving African financiers to tap domestic capital, as governments roll out policies, regulations and incentives to fund projects.

item.title: Neuberger Space Fund Turns to Europe as SpaceX Hype Buoys Sector
item.description: Evelyn Chow’s space-focused stock fund is scouring Europe for bargains just as the looming debut of SpaceX supercharges valuations in the US.

item.title: Anthropic, OpenAI Splurge on London Office Space in Leasing Wave
item.description: Artificial intelligence-linked companies ranging from Anthropic PBC to OpenAI have been rushing into London for office space, a surge that’s surprised local landlords struggling to boost the supply of top quality property.

item.title: Cities Need to Boost Green Investment for Safety, EIB Chief Says
item.description: Cities are increasing investment in the green transition to protect against the potentially catastrophic effects of climate change, the European Investment Bank president said.

item.title: Goldman, Nomura Cave on PBOC Stimulus After Politburo Restraint
item.description: Economists at Goldman Sachs Group Inc. and Nomura Holdings Inc. joined peers at other major global banks in abandoning calls for China’s monetary easing this year, as top leaders convey confidence in growth and little urgency to step up support.

item.title: Solaria Raises €300 Million to Fund Data Center, Battery Boost
item.description: Spanish clean-energy producer Solaria Energía y Medio Ambiente SA has raised about €300 million in a share offering, with the funds to be deployed in expanding its data center and battery storage operations.

item.title: Millennium, NBIM Are Said to Invest in CATL’s $5 Billion Share Sale
item.description: Hedge-fund firm Millennium Management and Norway’s sovereign-wealth fund Norges Bank Investment Management are among those that participated in battery maker Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd.’s $5 billion share sale, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Morocco Firm Plans First Fundraise for $25 Billion Gas Pipeline
item.description: Morocco’s state-controlled natural resources company plans to start raising part of the $25 billion needed to build a pipeline that will link gas fields in West Africa to the Mediterranean coastline.

item.title: Japan Confirms Hormuz Transit as Iran Talks Continue, PM Says
item.description: Japan’s prime minister confirmed that a ship linked to the country has transited the Strait of Hormuz, as Tokyo continues to lobby Tehran for the safe passage of all vessels through the chokepoint.

item.title: Crop Prices Hit Highest Since 2023 as War and Bad Weather Bite
item.description: The extended closure of the Strait of Hormuz and extreme weather have jolted the price index for farm commodities to a two-year high as fertilizer headaches and the prospect of smaller harvests drive food inflation risks.

item.title: European Airports Warn of Tough Outlook as War Disrupts Flights
item.description: Operators of Europe’s largest airports are warning of an increasingly uncertain outlook as the Middle East conflict adjusts flight schedules and restricts access to key airspaces.
